heading: Tamleugh North vs Tandarook — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tamleugh North, Victoria and Tandarook, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tamleugh North has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Tandarook (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tamleugh North or Tandarook safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tamleugh North has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Tandarook scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tamleugh North scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Tandarook scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tamleugh North or Tandarook?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tamleugh North has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
